title: ADP manifest
description: Root of an Action-data package (adp.manifest.json). Self-describing index of the package's logical parts, each resolvable from any storage back-end (BYOS). Carries no service metadata (owner/visibility/status live in the hosting service, not the package).

adp_version.description: ADP format version this manifest conforms to.

session_ref.description: Stable UUID of the logical activity. Survives the user appending new raw data later; identical across every part of this package.

generated_utc.description: When this manifest was generated (UTC, ISO 8601).

timezone.description: IANA timezone the activity was recorded in (e.g. Europe/Helsinki). Local-time context only; all timestamps in the package are UTC.

refine_version.description: Version of the refine/merge process that produced the layer-2 canonical parts.

parts.description: The logical parts that make up the package.

tiers.description: Packaging tiers: which parts belong to the shareable 'light' core vs the 'heavy' layer-1 originals. Values are `part` names present in parts[].

light.description: Shareable scrubbed core, egresses by default: typically session, timeline, geo, provenance, clips.

heavy.description: Layer-1 originals, usually stay at origin: typically raw, video.

scrub.description: Record of privacy/scrubbing applied when producing the egressing (layer-2/3) parts.

removed.description: Human-readable list of what was removed/generalized (e.g. 'home-radius GPS start/end', 'device serials').

part.description: Logical part name, unique within the manifest (e.g. session, timeline, geo, provenance, clips, raw, video, or an enrichment id).

layer.description: Which of the 3 layers this part belongs to (1 raw, 2 refined canonical, 3 enrichment).

role.description: Semantic role of the part; drives which per-part schema validates its payload.

format.description: Concrete on-storage format, e.g. json, parquet, geojson, gpx, fit, mp4.

fallback.description: Optional alternate representation of the same logical part (e.g. a csv mirror of a parquet timeline).

key.description: Storage key of the fallback, within the same store as `location`.

bytes.description: Size of the part's payload in bytes (omitted when not yet materialized).

hash.description: Content hash of the part's bytes, algorithm-prefixed (e.g. 'sha256:...'). Enables integrity checks and dedup across stores.

location.description: Where the part's bytes live. BYOS: any part may come from a different store.

store.description: 'default' = service default store; 'byos:<alias>' = user-supplied store resolved out-of-band; 'cold' = archival storage (may need rehydration).

key.description: Object key / path of the part within `store`.
